extraction.one_line_summary: BISSEGGER dominated the 9km Ajman ITT, winning by 7 seconds over GANNA despite the world champion starting 90+ minutes later in different conditions.
tactical_patterns: BISSEGGER held hot seat for 60+ minutes before GANNA started, suggesting early starters had better wind conditions.
POGAČAR gained 11 seconds on YATES Adam despite both sub-10-minute rides, positioning critical before mountain stage.
BJERG sub-10-minute time from early start proves EF tactics prioritize ITT specialists starting early for stage results.
DUMOULIN 14 seconds down on BISSEGGER shows post-layoff time trial form exceeds GC contention but lacks sharpness.

rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: positive: won by 7 seconds over world champion despite 60-minute gap showing peak form on short power ITTs
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: second place despite rainbow jersey suggests INEOS miscalculated start time for optimal wind conditions
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: gained 11 seconds on YATES Adam with third-quickest time, building GC buffer before Jebel Jais climb
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: second at checkpoint and sub-10:05 finish shows UAE depth in ITT threatens team tactics on GC stages
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: sub-10-minute ride post-layoff confirms return to elite time trialing despite 14-second deficit to winner
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: lost leader's jersey despite strong sprinter ITT performance, suggests UAE Tour ITT selects pure specialists
rider_signals.BJERG Mikkel: positive: sub-10-minute time from early start reveals EF prioritizes stage hunting over conserving ITT specialists
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: lost 11 seconds to POGAČAR despite excellent ITT exposes vulnerability before critical mountain stage
